summary: Implemented QUAL-001 with strict TDD flow in allowed files: added the `dedup` package export, implemented `SemanticDeduplicator` with `run`, `_fetch_candidates`, `_classify_pair`, `_merge_routes`, `emit_calibration_set`, runtime-budget warning in `main`, and a cost ledger; created a full pytest suite for AC-1 through AC-6 using mocked Convex HTTP via `responses`; and added required arbitration/calibration JSON artifacts. All QUAL-001 tests pass (6/6), but commit is blocked by pre-existing repository hook failures unrelated to this task (workspace install/link breakage and repo-wide TypeScript/Convex generation issues), so no new commit could be created without violating hook policy.

notebook_entries.action: Implemented QUAL-001 dedup engine and tests using RED→GREEN, then ran validations and attempted commit.
notebook_entries.outcome: Feature code and tests are complete with 6 passing tests; commit blocked by pre-existing hook/toolchain failures outside write-allowed scope.
notebook_entries.learning: QUAL-001 behavior is verified in isolation, but repository pre-commit currently depends on broken workspace links and missing/generated TS assets that prevent any commit.
